Health_Department_Name: Glenn County Health and Human Services Agency
Program_Name: Special Supplemental Nutrition Program for Women, Infants, and Children
Program_Description: The Special Supplemental Nutrition Program for Women, Infants, and Children (WIC) is a federal assistance program designed to support low-income pregnant women, breastfeeding mothers, infants, and young children up to the age of five.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant women, breastfeeding mothers, infants, and children up to age five

Health_Department_Name: Glenn County Health and Human Services Agency
Program_Name: Special Supplemental Nutrition Program for Women, Infants, and Children
Program_Description: The WIC program provides nutritional support and education to low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women, as well as infants and children.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children

Health_Department_Name: Glenn County Health and Human Services Agency
Program_Description: WIC is a program that provides supplemental foods for low-income breastfeeding or expectant women and their preschool children.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children under 5 years.
Eligibility_Requirements: Must meet low-income criteria and health assessment by a professional.
Application_Process: Contact your state agency to reserve an appointment.
